title: monero: address_for_session 31-bit mask causes birthday collision at ~65k sessions
description: MoneroKeys::address_for_session (nie-monero/src/lib.rs line 139) derives the subaddress minor index from only the first 4 bytes of the UUID masked to 31 bits (& 0x7FFF_FFFF), giving 2^31 approx 2.1 billion distinct values. By birthday paradox, at ~65,000 sessions the probability of two sessions sharing a subaddress address reaches ~0.1%; at ~46,000 sessions the cumulative probability exceeds 50%. Two sessions that map to the same minor index receive the same subaddress. A payment to either session activates that address, making it impossible for the wallet to determine which session was paid. The 0x7FFF_FFFF mask is unnecessarily restrictive: Monero minor index is a full u32, giving 2^32 values if the mask is removed. Fix: remove the 31-bit mask so the full 32-bit UUID prefix is used for the minor index, doubling the collision-free session capacity.
